introduction: As we continue to explore innovative sales approaches, we are considering the potential of a direct-to-customer model. This presentation will examine the potential benefits and implications of this shift on our sales and margins.

subsections.heading: Understanding Electric Vehicle Market Trends
subsections.content:
• Observations from Q1 2025 show a notable increase in sales among our primary competitors who have adopted the direct-to-customer selling model.
• This increase can be attributed to the elimination of dealership markups and fees, which have been reported to reach up to $20,000 per vehicle.
• The direct-to-customer strategy not only increases margins but also provides the opportunity to control the end-to-end customer experience.
• Considering the substantial revenues of dealership groups, transitioning to a direct sales model could potentially redirect a significant portion of those revenues to manufacturers.
• A well-executed transition could position Elexion as a leader in the EV market by combining our commitment to sustainability and user-centric technology with a more customer-friendly sales model.
• It's crucial to develop a robust and efficient sales system capable of handling the complexities of direct customer interactions.
• Careful monitoring and analysis of competitor performance can provide valuable insights and help in refining our sales strategy.
subsections.justification: These points highlight the success of competitors who switched to direct sales and the potential benefits for Elexion, providing a basis for answering the DR Question.

subsections.heading: Transitioning to Direct-to-Customer Sales: Prospects and Challenges
subsections.content:
• Traditional automotive retail model involves franchised dealerships which act as intermediaries between automakers and consumers
• Direct-to-consumer model eliminates the need for dealership intermediaries, potentially leading to cost savings
• Challenges may include regulatory hurdles, logistical issues, and customer service concerns
• Direct sales model could offer greater control over the customer experience
• However, transitioning to this model may require significant investments in IT infrastructure
• Disintermediation also poses risks, such as increased responsibility for customer service and after-sales care
subsections.justification: This content outlines the general prospects and challenges of transitioning to direct sales, but does not specifically address how Elexion could capture benefits or drive higher margins.

subsections.heading: Potential Impact on Elexion's Sales and Margins
subsections.content:
• Moving to a direct sales model could potentially increase Elexion's gross margins by eliminating dealer markups
• It could also improve Elexion's control over pricing and inventory
• However, the transition could also present significant up-front costs, potentially impacting short-term profitability
• Direct sales could improve customer experience and brand perception, potentially driving long-term sales growth
• Elexion would also need to manage after-sales service, which could affect margins
• Elexion's financial performance will depend on various factors, including the successful implementation of the direct sales model, market conditions, and regulatory environment
subsections.justification: This content discusses the potential impacts of a sales model shift on Elexion, but does not provide specific strategies for capturing benefits or increasing margins in 2027.

conclusion: In conclusion, transitioning to a direct-to-customer sales model could potentially drive higher sales and margins. Careful planning, extensive market research, and cross-functional collaboration will be critical for a successful shift.
